docs.title: Order Service
docs.text:
The Order Service creates and lists authenticated customer orders.

It persists order data in PostgreSQL, validates products by calling Product Service and converts totals through Exchange Service when a different currency is requested.

docs.title: Responsibilities
docs.text:
• Create orders for the current account.
  • Store order items and totals.
  • List only orders that belong to the current account.
  • Convert order totals using the Exchange API.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    Client[Gateway] --> Resource[Order Resource]
    Resource --> Service[Order Service]
    Service --> Product[Product Service]
    Service --> Exchange[Exchange Service]
    Service --> Repository[Order Repository]
    Repository --> Database[(PostgreSQL)]

docs.title: Service Calls
docs.text: OpenFeign clients call Product Service and Exchange Service using URLs injected through PRODUCT_SERVICE_URL and EXCHANGE_SERVICE_URL.

docs.title: Bottlenecks
docs.text: Order creation depends on Product Service availability. Currency conversion also depends on Exchange Service when the client requests a currency different from USD.

docs.title: Mitigations
docs.text:
• Short HTTP timeouts on OpenFeign.
  • Defensive error mapping for unavailable dependencies.
  • Kubernetes HPA for CPU-based scale-out.
  • PostgreSQL schema managed by Flyway to keep deployments repeatable.

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text:
Base URL in local compose: http://localhost:8081.

All order endpoints require the id-account header.

Method Path Description GET / Health check POST /orders Create an order GET /orders List account orders GET /orders/{id} Get order details

docs.title: Repositories
docs.text: Component Repository Platform aggregator micro_api_26.1 Gateway Service gateway-service Auth facade auth Auth Service auth-service Account facade account Account Service account-service Product Service product-service Order Service order-service Exchange Service exchange
